title: Volume mounting does not work in bash on Windows
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)** /kind bug **Description** After installing Podman Desktop on Windows, a local HTML file is opened with the following information:

Volume Mounting

New in Podman v4.1 is the ability to perform volume mounts from Windows paths into a Linux container. This supports several notation schemes, including: Windows Style Paths: `podman run --rm -v c:\Users\User\myfolder:/myfolder ubi8-micro ls /myfolder` Unixy Windows Paths: `podman run --rm -v /c/Users/User/myfolder:/myfolder ubi8-micro ls /myfolder` Linux paths local to the WSL filesystem: `podman run --rm -v /var/myfolder:/myfolder ubi-micro ls /myfolder` All of the above conventions work, whether running on a Windows prompt or the WSL Linux shell. Although when using Windows paths on Linux, appropriately quote or escape the Windows path portion of the argument.
Running these example commands fails in bash on Windows. **Steps to reproduce the issue:** 1. Install [git-for-windows](https://gitforwindows.org/) or [MSYS2](https://www.msys2.org/) to get a native Windows build of bash. 2. In bash, create a subdirectory named `myfolder` in your home and put a file into it: ```bash $ cd ~ $ mkdir myfolder $ touch myfolder/hello.txt ``` 3. Run the example command, replacing your username as necessary, e.g.: ```bash $ podman run --rm -v /c/Users/$(whoami)/myfolder:/myfolder ubi8-micro ls /myfolder ``` 3. The error message shown below is seen and the container fails to start. **Describe the results you received:** In git bash: ```bash $ podman run --rm -v /c/Users/$(whoami)/myfolder:/myfolder ubi8-micro ls /myfolder Error: invalid container path "\\Program Files\\Git\\myfolder", must be an absolute path ``` In MSYS2: ```bash $ podman run --rm -v /c/Users/$(whoami)/myfolder:/myfolder ubi8-micro ls /myfolder Error: invalid container path "\\msys64\\myfolder", must be an absolute path ``` And with Windows Style Paths in git bash: ```bash $ podman run --rm -v C:\\Users\\$(whoami)\\myfolder:/myfolder ubi8-micro ls /myfolder ls: cannot access 'C:/Program Files/Git/myfolder': No such file or directory ``` Windows Style Paths in MSYS2: ```bash $ podman run --rm -v C:\\Users\\$(whoami)\\myfolder:/myfolder ubi8-micro ls /myfolder ls: cannot access 'C:/msys64/myfolder': No such file or directory ``` For some reason, the container path is being interpreted somewhere and ends up becoming a Windows path? Even when the argument is single-quoted so the shell will not attempt to interpret it as anything other than a string. **Describe the results you expected:** Running the example commands works correctly in Windows Command Prompt and PowerShell. It also works with Cmder, which I believe is just cmd under the hood. For instance, this was in PowerShell: ```powershell > podman run --rm -v /c/Users/$env:UserName/myfolder:/myfolder ubi8-micro ls /myfolder hello.txt ``` **Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** **Output of `podman version`:** ``` $ podman version Client: Podman Engine Version: 4.2.1 API Version: 4.2.1 Go Version: go1.16.15 Git Commit: 62b324ddf718411b1d4d0ba8117c632f7f984a38 Built: Wed Sep 7 06:08:04 2022 OS/Arch: windows/amd64 Server: Podman Engine Version: 4.2.1 API Version: 4.2.1 Go Version: go1.18.5 Built: Wed Sep 7 12:58:19 2022 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 ``` **Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)** Yes I just downloaded and installed Podman for the first time today. **Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):** The issue appears to be specific to bash on Windows, which is not an officially supported Windows shell. I use bash in Windows Terminal, but it isn't necessary to reproduce the problem; It also happens in the terminal emulator provided by the `git-for-windows` installer. Which is `mintty` 3.6.1, FWIW. I'm not sure which terminal emulator MSYS2 uses (also looks like mintty 3.6.1). This is the version of Bash provided in git-for-windows: ```bash $ bash --version GNU bash, version 5.1.16(1)-release (x86_64-pc-msys) Copyright (C) 2020 Free Software Foundation, Inc. License GPLv3+: GNU GPL version 3 or later This is free software; you are free to change and redistribute it. There is NO WARRANTY, to the extent permitted by law. ``` And it looks identical in MSYS2: ```bash $ bash --version GNU bash, version 5.1.16(1)-release (x86_64-pc-msys) Copyright (C) 2020 Free Software Foundation, Inc. License GPLv3+: GNU GPL version 3 or later This is free software; you are free to change and redistribute it. There is NO WARRANTY, to the extent permitted by law. ```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@parasyte Sorry this one fell through the cracks. What's happening here is that git bash is autoconverting root paths for arguments to point to the c drive location bash is setup to emulate, so its actually "/folder" that is the source of the problem. 

There is a couple solutions. One is you can prepend an additional slash like //folder. Another is you can call shell like so:

`podman run --rm -v C:\\Users\\$(whoami)\\myfolder:/myfolder ubi8-micro sh -c 'ls /myfolder'`

I'm going to close this since it doesn't look like a podman bug, but feel free to reopen if I missed something.

Thanks!
Thanks @n1hility, that does work. I still don't know why "Unixy Windows Paths" do not work in git bash. E.g. `/c/Users/...` style paths and `$PWD` give the error shown in the OP.
@parasyte ah right, on that, you need a colon after the drive letter like this: `/c:/Users'.
Ok! I can work around this issue with something like: `$(echo $PWD | sed 's#^/c/#/c:/#')` to add the colon.
